reviews.text: In my search for a divorce lawyer, after our first conversation I thought this is the guy. He stood out from the others I interviewed. A year later my case is now over and I'm grateful to have gotten the chance to work with Mr. Ferris.  He has guided me and given me excellent advice through out this whole process. Mr. Ferris has been doing this a long time and has tons of experience. I sit today in my happy place, my kids are with me, we have a roof over our heads and life is good. I have Mr. Ferris to thank for helping me get here.

Updated Review as of 2026

I am incredibly grateful for the exceptional legal support I received from Scott during my divorce six years ago. He was there for me during a very difficult time, demonstrating remarkable attention to detail and dedication to my case. His professionalism and empathy made a challenging experience much more manageable.Recently, I sought Scott's assistance again when my daughter was struggling in school and facing mental health challenges. With his expert guidance, I successfully obtained full custody of my daughter, who is now thriving with straight A's and perfect attendance. This turnaround has been nothing short of life-changing for both of us.What sets Scott apart is his unwavering commitment to his clients. He is consistently attentive and responsive, available via text, calls, and emails 24/7, even when he is on vacation. This level of accessibility is rare in the legal field, and it has provided me with immense peace of mind.I’ve heard numerous horror stories about attorneys who take your money and leave you feeling scared and unsupported. My experience with Scott has been the complete opposite. Even during moments of disagreement, he fought tirelessly and successfully for my best interests, ultimately helping me achieve the most favorable outcome.I highly recommend Scott to anyone in need of a family court attorney. I have referred him to several friends, all of whom have reported positive experiences and successful outcomes in their cases, thanks to Scott's thorough and prompt approach.Thank you, Scott, for your unwavering support and expertise!

My review of Scott Ferris - May 2020

Where to  begin. How do you express such a magnitude of gratitude that I have for Scott Ferris.

When I hired Scott, I was in pieces. I was fresh out of a very bad marriage and did not feel I had the strength for the road ahead.  My mom hired Scott for me, in the very beginning of the pandemic.  My world and the literal world were coming to and end in my eyes.  Scott made absolute miracles happen in what was the ugliest and darkest of times for me.  By no means was my divorce a clear cut, easy peasy, everyone is making things go smoothly situation.  Scott was not only the most amazing attorney I could have asked for, but he truly supported me 100%.  He had my back 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.  He answered my questions, my e-mails, my texts, my crying calls... ALWAYS.  He was the voice of reason when I couldn't see any options. He was my backbone when I needed to be tough and didn't think I had it in me.  He taught me how to go about the end of my marriage and come out with a clear, level head.  He taught me the importance of co-parenting and doing it without anger and spite. He was invaluable and I cannot stress this enough.  I was divorced, with my head held high, and my family on the road to healing,... within 5 months of filing, and with the courts practically shut down due to Covid-19.  It really was an absolute miracle in my eyes.

It has been about 6 months since my divorce was final and I still have Scott's support and guidance to this day.  He takes the time to check up on me, my mother and my daughter.  He takes the time to answer me every single time I need him, without fail.

You could not possibly be in better hands if you choose to have Scott Ferris represent you. I would make the choice over and over again! (but I hope never to need to.)

I Came to Mr.Ferris with a serious criminal matter which I was also sued for civilly. Mr.Ferris cleverly disposed of the civil suit first circumventing the victim's criminal case against me. Both were resolved favorably. Mr.Ferris returns his calls and texts promptly. You can reach him almost any time of day. Makes you feel like his most important client.

I highly recommend Scott Ferris. He came recommended by another one of his clients, and I’m so glad I went with him as my attorney. His services aren’t the most inexpensive around, but worth every penny. We were able to settle in mediation, which saved me plenty of additional fees. Ferris is experienced, reliable, responsive, logical, and will fight for you even when you feel like giving up. He is straightforward, to the point, and won’t sugarcoat things.

Having Scott working on my side, I can confidently say he exceeded every expectation. From the very beginning, dedication, and attention to detail. He took the time to truly understand my situation, guided me through every step, and always made sure I felt informed and supported.

Thanks to his expertise and persistence, my case was resolved.— something I’ll always be grateful for. What stood out most was his honesty, responsiveness.
It’s rare to find an attorney will care about his work but also communicates with such clarity and respect. He was also very straight forward and direct.

I highly recommend Scott Ferris to anyone looking for a knowledgeable and trustworthy attorney who truly delivers results.

I cannot say enough positive things about my experience with Scott Ferris. He has only been on my case for about a month, but in that short time, he has done more than I ever expected. My case involves very difficult issues, including parental alienation, custody, and relocation, and Scott has handled everything with remarkable skill and dedication.

He is extremely aggressive when it comes to advocating for his clients, yet always precise and professional in his approach. He doesn’t miss a detail, communicates clearly, and makes sure I understand every step of the process. Most importantly, he has given me confidence during one of the most stressful times of my life by showing that he genuinely cares!

Scott Ferris is an outstanding attorney who goes above and beyond for his clients. I am beyond grateful to have him representing me and would highly recommend him to anyone in need of a strong, knowledgeable, and dedicated lawyer.

I came to Scott during a very difficult time, unsure of what the future held for my marriage. While I ultimately chose to stay married, working with Scott was one of the best decisions I made. He gave me the strength, clarity, and confidence I needed to navigate a complex emotional situation with a clear mind and sense of empowerment.

Scott was compassionate, honest, and deeply knowledgeable. He helped me fully understand my rights as a woman and a spouse, which made all the difference in how I approached my relationship moving forward. He never pressured me in any direction—just supported me with facts, perspective, and calm reassurance.

What stood out most was Scott’s deep knowledge of family law and his remarkable flexibility. I was traveling during this time, yet he always found a way to keep me informed, prepared, and empowered—even making sure I was ready for court when that seemed like a major obstacle. He communicated with consistency, patience, and genuine care, always making my safety and peace of mind a top priority.

Even though I didn’t go through with a divorce, I walked away from this experience stronger, more informed, and deeply grateful for Scott’s guidance.

I would recommend Scott to anyone in need of a strong, compassionate, and skilled attorney. Even today, I feel safer knowing I can turn to him if I ever need guidance again. Having him in my corner made all the difference.

Scott Ferris is exceptional! From start to finish, he was professional, knowledgeable, and incredibly responsive. He went above and beyond to make sure everything was handled smoothly and efficiently. You can tell he genuinely cares about his clients and takes pride in his work. Highly recommend Scott if you want someone reliable, honest, and easy to work with!

When we were in what we thought was an impossible custody and relocation situation and had lost all hope, we were referred to Scott, it became clear quickly why he came so highly recommended. Scott demonstrated an attentive approach, ensuring he understood our family's needs and advocating for our best interests. Despite the difficult dynamics with the other parent, he remained professional, ethical, and focused on our well-being, offering unwavering support and representation.

Choosing Scott Ferris as your representative ensures that you are in capable hands. He is dedicated to advocating for his clients and navigating them through difficult situations to achieve the best possible outcome. Even after the final judgment, Scott continues to provide support and check in on our family, demonstrating his ongoing commitment to our well-being.

Scott gave my family exceptional support and guidance during our father’s divorce proceedings. At 93, navigating such a significant life change is daunting, but Scott approached the situation with compassion and professionalism and it was clear that he genuinely cared about my father’s well-being.

Scott took the time to explain every step of the process in a way that was easy to understand, ensuring that my father, and we, felt informed and comfortable with each decision. He was always available to answer questions, no matter how small. He looked out for my father’s best interests and the outcome was much more positive than we anticipated.

I highly recommend him to anyone seeking a skilled attorney who cares about their clients. Thank you, Scott!

I came to Scott with an impossible divorce case. It was 2-1/2 years old, the previous attorney had multiple health issues and had grossly under-litigated my case. In 3 months time he got all the depositions done, 2 case management hearings, motions heard a mediation, and was ready for trial. Scott did more in 3 months than the other attorney did in 2-1/2 years! I wish I would have started with Scott Ferris!

If you need a good attorney, save yourself years of grief and headaches   Scott worked hard for me and I had fantastic results! Give Scott a call. He’s got your back.

. I recently had the pleasure of working with the Scott Ferris and his team and wanted to express my gratitude for the exceptional legal services they provided. Scott was incredible to work with, he quickly acted upon the issues I brought him and we were able to get things resolved in a timely & professional manner. He cares for his clients and will go above and beyond to make sure you’re comfortable & your issues are dealt with.

Despite my ex-wife choosing a law firm to represent her who I found to be less than ethical, Scott kept me cool headed every time the opposing council did things that would cause me to lose my mind. My divorce turned out to be very difficult and contentious, and Scott was always there for me. He became someone to lean on not just as an advisor, but also as a friend. Scott was always available to me, even for stupid questions at odd hours, and always kept all of my concerns, including cost, in mind. He helped me to get through a very difficult time, and still keep peace of mind. He didn't always tell me exactly what I wanted to hear, but always had my best interests at heart. I would recommend his services without reservation. You won't be sorry.

His assistant, Jose Diemer, was also extremely helpful by communicating immediately and providing assistance with required documents. Mr. Ferris did WIN my case: However, even if the results would have been different, I would still stand by my review.  I highly recommend!  Thank you Scott !

I was accused of a crime I did not commit.  A 1st degree felony with a maximum sentence of 30yrs!  I always kinda thought that this charge would somehow go away but it never did.  It only got more and more serious as time went on.  Literally, no-one wanted to hear my story until I met Attorney Lett.  He not only believed that I should not of been charged with a 1st degree felony, he actually began to build a coherent strategy to fight it.  On the day of trial, the prosecutor was still committed to prosecute my case and send me to prison for a long time.  Attorney Lett kept his cool and kept going forward with his strategy to intelligently show the jury that I was not guilty.  After the jury was paneled and after our opening statements, the next day, the prosecutor agreed to lower my charge to a misdemeanor, adjudication withheld, with 1 year probation suspended after 6 months!  Needless to say, everyones jaw dropped including the judge as I went from a 1st degree felony facing 30 years to a simple misdemeanor.  I would encourage anyone who is up very serious charges and want an honest answer and a listening ear to call Ariel Lett.  You will not be disappointed Im sure.

I had (what I thought was) a very interesting opportunity involving a non-US company. I was referred to Mr. Molina and contacted his office.  We worked on this in two phases:  the first was a due diligence document and the second was a proposed contract.

The turnaround time was reasonable and the cost was manageable.

The office is rigidly structured in terms of their communications which at first was a bit off putting but in the end, it's what make them efficient, I think.  (Send an email, someone will call you.  But they DO call you.)

At any rate, I would use them again.

Edit:

reviews.ownerResponse:
We take every review seriously, positive or negative. Knowing all the facts, we feel this review does not accurately portray what happened on this case. Unfortunately, due to attorney/client confidentiality, we cannot post the omitted info online. Without breaching any confidences, we will say a few things.

With respect to your comments concerning the recovery and our decision not to pursue your case any further, sadly it is not always an option. Without comprehensive coverage sufficient to cover the loss or damage, we are limited in our ability to recover the full measure of your damages. What I mean by that is - Florida is currently the only state that does not require Bodily Injury (BI) coverage for drivers allowing tens of thousands of drivers on the road to be underinsured besides the significant percentage of drivers on our roads with no insurance at all.

This means it is legal in Florida for anyone to drive a vehicle that has no Bodily Injury (BI) insurance. The minimal coverage required in our state provides NO compensation directly to an injured person for their injuries caused by an automobile accident. Further, for those that do possess BI coverage, there are no limitations required by the State of Florida, which means that many drivers are woefully underinsured. This is alarming and understandably frustrating for people to discover; especially if you were recently injured by a vehicle or suffered a loss by a vehicle that did not have comprehensive coverage. Unless the victim carries their own Uninsured (Underinsured) Motorist coverage they are left with little or no recourse.

We are terribly sorry for your loss and understand your frustration and anger as a grieving parent. We invest significant time and resources necessary to investigate the circumstances of each client's case and are committed to holding those responsible accountable for their wrongdoing, even if we ultimately receive no compensation in the end due to circumstances out of our control. This was particularly true in your case.

We understand that our clients trust us with their future, and we make every effort to repay that trust each and every day by providing compassionate, relentless, and aggressive representation, by protecting individual rights, by delivering trusted results through monetary compensation, and by ensuring these corporations and individuals are held accountable for their wrongdoing. We aim to be the best at litigating cases involving devastating injuries and wrongful death.

We've proudly represented our clients with integrity, diligence, and kindness, and I am sorry that was not your experience.

Thank God the bank returned my money; I didn't lose anything. You are in Florida, and I'm in California. I tried to resolve this amicably, but they stopped answering my calls. BE CAREFUL; IF YOU DON'T PAY, THEY WILL REPORT YOU.

Scam... Don't fall for it. They gave me a free initial consultation and told me my case qualified for representation, then made me pay $250, which I paid without hesitation. I had a second interview with a paralegal where I explained everything in more detail because they were supposedly preparing the case file for the lawyer. Four days later (Thursday at 3 pm) was the appointment with the lawyer. The day arrived, and he didn't call me. I waited a few hours and called, but only got an answering machine. I looked on the website and saw another phone number, which I called. The secretary answered and told me the lawyer wasn't going to take the case. I saw there are other similar scams when I read the reviews. People, don't fall into the hands of these individuals. There are surely qualified lawyers out there, but this one clearly isn't.

I attended an appointment three days ago with lawyer García Hernandez. I explained to him my case at Floor & Decor in Doral, Miami, as an employed part-time for a year, although letting them know that I was willing to be employed full-time but it was denied on several occations while other co-workers with less time and experience were prompted to full-time, even before their three-month probation, I have proof about it, but Garcia did not want to read it. I also added other incidents from the Store's managers, and Human Resource Department who showed complicity in denying to me more hours and benefits. Most relevant Garcia's responses were: " In other States, there is more protection for employees, but in Florida, the Employer is the most, and Human Resources protects its business"; " You should follow your employer's orders without making such a fuss to avoid this king of stupidity"; and, "After listening to everything you have mentioned, there is nothing we can help with". According to him who abides by the laws of Florida in regarding to this, he becomes an accomplice among those who do not do anything in favor, ignoring my damages. If there is no protection on the above, I hope that a campaign can be raised calling for more laws that defend the right to employee benefits expanding the recognition of labor discrimination. In conclusion,  Garcia's lawyer was disrespectful to me calling my case as stupidity.

ZERO STARS!!

This is my experience with Arguelles Legal in Coral Gables which I found unprofessional, dishonest, and deeply negligent in representing my interests.

They presented themselves as fully available and highly competent when they were negotiating my signature on a representation contract, yet their promises proved hollow.
I felt pressured into signing a contract for representation with the appearance of urgency and certainty. There was no clear disclosure of the risks, no written explanation of their fees in plain terms, and no honest discussion about expected outcomes or timelines.

Without my explicit consent or even a discussion of the consequences, they opened claims with my insurance. This was done in my name, without my informed authorization or understanding of what it would entail.
When I later pressed for explanations, they could not justify these actions beyond vague assurances, which I consider an egregious breach of trust and professional duty.

After I signed, their communication dropped to near-nothing. Attempts to reach them were met with silence, delays, and a sense that my case was no longer a priority.
There was no proactive follow-up, no updates, and no accountability for the promises they had made at the outset.

When I finally managed to reach them via email through the receptionist, I was confronted with a later claim that two lawsuit claims had been opened against my insurance and the at-fault driver’s insurer, and that they had dropped my representation because they “couldn’t reach me.”
This explanation is nothing short of a contradiction and, frankly, a fabrication: I received no calls, no emails, and no voicemails from them. There was no attempt to verify contact details, no documented attempts to reach me, and no legitimate basis to disengage from representing me.

I now have two open cases and must find a new attorney, wasting time, money, and emotional energy on remedying the fallout from their incompetence and misrepresentation.
Their conduct has created a complicated legal situation that I did not consent to and that far exceeds ordinary service failures; it feels like both neglect and deception.

Bottom line
If you value transparency, proactive communication, and ethical handling of your insurance and legal matters, you should avoid Arguelles Law Firm.  What I experienced was deceptive onboarding, unilateral actions without consent, and a total lack of professional accountability that left me vulnerable and underserved.

Sometimes we don't realize that what we don't know can hurt us.  Being married and having a property without a prenuptial agreement and this  property being the "homestead", as Mrs Mendez explained,  spouse has rights even if he is not on the deed.

Martha Mendez explained all the scenarios and she was able to generate the documentation in favor of all of us.

So, I highly recommend Martha  Mendez  at Feistein and Mendez for everyone's legal needs.

Best regards,

Suzann M.

reviews.text: Customer Service? Nil and none, I called because my aunt is suffering,  was placed in a nursing home by mistake, need counsel to get her out, he answer the phone very rudely, made me sent him the power of attorney never from him again. Wouldn't recommend him to my dog! Horrible, rude person! 😱
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your feedback. I’m sorry to hear that your experience was not what you had hoped for.

As an attorney governed by the Florida Bar's ethical rules I am limited in my reply to your public reviews. Per my own practice, I confirm that anyone inquiring about another person’s legal matter has proper authority before I can discuss details or consider offering any representation.

To summarize my recollection of our interaction, I picked up an inquiry call you made to my office and spoke to you immediately seeing how I could possibly be of assistance. I did not provide you or your aunt any legal advice, I was never formally engaged, and no attorney-client relationship was established between us. Our communication was limited to that brief call and an email exchange, and receipt of a non-accessible link to what may have been a document you referenced over a month ago. Communication ended there.

I strive to treat all inquiries with professionalism and empathy - especially in sensitive family situations like the one you describe. I take all feedback seriously, and I am continually working to ensure every interaction reflects my high standard of service. I’m proud of the many reviews (60+ 5-star reviews) from clients I’ve had the privilege to assist. While I welcome constructive feedback, I do wish you would have reached out again so I could have addressed your concerns directly rather than airing our brief interaction online and becoming one of your reviews online.

I sincerely wish you and your aunt strength during this difficult time and I hope you’ve found the right support and counsel for your matter.

reviews.text:
My brothers and I placed our mother in the nursing home, the social workers at the nursing home are great professionals dedicated to help the patient’s family, diligently applied to Medicaid on my mother’s behalf. I am not disclosing the names of the social workers to protect them. Medicaid denied our case twice with a generic reason. The social workers requested DCF to elaborate but unsuccessful.  I also emailed the DCF representative, they never responded. The social worker referred me to Steven Linde, Esq. Thank you for referring us to Steven Linde,Esq.
I reached out to Steven Linde, Esq. I explained we had two denials, but couldn’t get responses to cure whatever the DCF was referring to. Steven immediately, without an attorney agreement contacted the social worker to get better informed of the case and then he contacted DCF. After a few days talking back and forth with Steven we signed his agreement. Within two weeks Steven got our family the Medicaid approval for my mother.
In my experience with attorneys’, when an attorney with passion works for you, the client receives better results. I say Passion is the key for better results to the client. Steven is a passionate attorney that measures success by helping the client’s needs first, second to his fees. We are very pleased with his services and his fee. It is very hard to find passion in many professionals today.
I will recommend Steven anytime for Elder Law.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Oscar. So glad to have played a small role in helping your mother get the benefits she desperately needed. All my best!

reviews.text:
The Best Legal Representation!!!

I cannot thank the Law Offices of Brian Breiter enough, especially David Chonin, for the incredible support and legal expertise they provided during one of the most traumatic times in my life. I was viciously attacked by four pit bulls while walking in Miami—a horrifying experience that left me physically and emotionally scarred.

From the very beginning, David Chonin was not only a fierce advocate but also a compassionate and steady presence throughout the entire process. He took the time to explain every step, always kept me informed, and made sure I felt supported at all times. His communication was impeccable—he was always available, responsive, and genuinely cared about my recovery and my rights.

Thanks to David’s determination and legal skill, he was able to recover every bit of insurance coverage available to me. He fought tirelessly on my behalf and ensured I received the justice and compensation I deserved.

If you ever find yourself in need of a personal injury attorney, I definitely recommend David Chonin and the entire team at the Law Offices of Brian Breiter. Their dedication, professionalism, and humanity truly set them apart.
reviews.ownerResponse: Nathalee I know it’s been a while since your father’s passing. And I’m sure you must feel the loss even to this day like it was yesterday. It was such an honor to represent you and your sister in this very important place and I’m glad we were able to resolve it for you to your satisfaction. Please stay in touch.

reviews.text:
Life-Changing Representation After My Motorcycle Accident

I can’t say enough good things about attorneys David Chonin and Brian Breiter. I was riding my motorcycle on Alton Road in Miami Beach on my way to work when a Bobcat tractor suddenly turned into me, leaving me with a broken leg and a long road to recovery. It was a terrifying, painful experience but David and Brian made sure I never faced it alone.

David Chonin came to the hospital to meet me personally something I’ll never forget. From that first moment, I knew I was in the hands of people who genuinely cared. Throughout the entire legal process, David was always available to answer my questions, no matter how small or complex. He kept me informed, reassured, and focused when things felt overwhelming.

I chose the Law Offices of Brian Breiter because they are experienced trial lawyers that are not afraid to try cases. The insurance companies know this. Brian and David worked tirelessly on my case, preparing the case for trial. On the eve of trial, they secured a settlement far beyond what I ever expected. Their dedication, legal expertise, and strategic approach made all the difference.

If you’re injured and don’t know where to turn, I wholeheartedly recommend this team. They’re not just great lawyers they’re compassionate advocates who will fight for you like family.

Thank you, David and Brian, for everything.
reviews.ownerResponse: Eduardo, thank you so much for the very kind review. David and I are so happy that we were able to get such a great settlement for you. Despite everything that happened to you, I believe that the result was fair under the circumstances. Thank you for trusting us and letting us go all the way. Stay in touch.

The insurance company wanted to give us less but our attorneys stood their ground in the number that was fair for our family’s loss. We ended up receiving a fair amount due to these attorneys.

reviews.text: Where to begin. What a journey it has been for our family’s wrongful death case. I can confidently say, to have David and Brian as our trial attorneys was a blessing. These men were emotionally supportive as well informative.  With their knowledge, we were able to ask those uncertain questions and always get a timely response. Our family never felt like we were kept out of the loop with what was going on during the duration of our case. Our case did go to trial where the insurance company wasn’t offering a fair amount, until these sharks in the courtroom demanded it with fair reasoning. They fought for what is fair without wavering and kept such a strong stance. If you want to be put in trusted hands especially during such a vulnerable time, they are attorneys that will fight for justice in your case.
reviews.ownerResponse: Nicole it was an honor representing your family along with David Chonin. Your father was a great and loving man. I am glad we were able to get Justice for him in the courtroom. May his memory be a blessing to all that knew him.

reviews.text: Brian Breiter's reputation as a top-rated personal injury attorney is well-deserved and anyone looking for an attorney who knows how to effectively fight for them should look no further than Brian Breiter.
reviews.ownerResponse: Robert, thank you for the kind words. We appreciate them.
Gracias

reviews.text:
A couple of years ago it was a beautiful day in Los Angeles and I was driving to the airport, next thing I know I’m in a horrible accident and my life is turned upside down. I’ve never been in a crash like this before, and I knew it was serious. It was obvious I was going to need legal help so I started searching online and asking friends. After doing tons of research I narrowed my search down to two law firms, the Law Offices of Brian Breiter and Movagar and Yamin.

I met with both firms and was impressed with each of them. When I interviewed them, I asked what their opinions were of the other firm, and they both said the same thing, great lawyers you can’t go wrong. I found out after talking with Brian Breiter that Nick Movagar used to work with him and that they often work on cases together. At that point Brian suggested that both firms could work together on the case, and that’s exactly what happened. I thought how can I lose, I get two lawyers for the price of one. Nick was amazing at helping me get access to the best doctors. I ended up needing a serious back surgery, and he found one of the best spine surgeons in Los Angeles.

The recovery was hard, but Brian and Nick made it easy. After the surgery they both arranged to have a nurse take care of me at home so I wouldn’t be alone. Nick arranged for a great physical therapy and after care.

Because the injuries were so serious and my medical bills were high the case had to go into litigation. Nicks firm handled the initial part of the case. When it became obvious that the insurance company was not going to pay what I deserved, Brain took over and prepared the case for the trial.

I felt in great hands with both firms. Nick and Brian made themselves available to me anytime. I had questions, a lot, and would sometimes text them on the weekends or in the middle of the night and they were always there to reassure me. The time came for the trial, but just before that the defense probably got scared and agreed to go to mediation. It was a long hard negotiation and Brian was there the whole way. He was a great negotiator, and we were able to settle the case for a fantastic and very fair outcome.

At the end of the case, Nick was amazing, he made sure that my medical bills, which were very high because I did not have insurance, were reduced so that I was left with a large part of the settlement. I really appreciated how hard he fought for me. It was a hard choice choosing between these two great attorneys, so I chose them both. I was lucky they agreed to work together, they make a great team. Thank you guys for taking such good care of me! 5 star service.

I was hit by a car walking in a crosswalk in Beverly Hills, CA. It happened almost 2 years ago. Engaging Brian and his team shortly thereafter was one of my best decisions ever.

His knowledge, and confidence told me I was working with the best professionals I could have found.

Brian's guidance was invaluable. He listened and helped me throughout the case. We were both patient and that patience produced positive results.

Give Brian a call immediately when the need for a great lawyer comes up in your life!

Brian has been a fantastic attorney. He's smart, friendly, and he gets results.  I took a nasty fall in a theater. When my previous attorney advised me that he felt my case was not worth pursuing, Brian took it and netted me a substantial settlement.  I have been in several unfortunate car accidents - I live in Los angeles, which is like a giant bumper car game - and he has, each time, helped me negotiate the sometimes excruciating gauntlet of insurance company obstacles.  Brian is professional, expert, personable, and a tiger in negotiation. I will use him again

When I was first looking for help with my bike accident I really couldn't believe that a lawyer could have 100% 5 star reviews.

But now I understand why. Brian and his team genuinely car for every single one of their clients.

At every step of the process of resolving the claim for my accident Brian and his team have been incredible - from making sure I got proper treatment, through talking me through the claim process and what to expect, through the multiple stages of the claim they have always been there and always been friendly and professional. My calls are always returned. My emails answered.

I can not thank them enough.

If you are unfortunate enough to be involved in an accident call Brian first.

My daughter was only 11 years old when her father was involved in a motorcycle accident with a vehicle belonging to the City of Los Angeles. David remained in a coma for over six months and died on my daughter's birthday from Traumatic Brain Injury.
The evening of the accident, my brother gave me a number of an attorney he had heard about and told me to call immediately. I'm so glad I called that same night. Overwhelmed and numb, I talked with Brian for hours on the phone, still in shock, explaining what had happened to David that morning and the delicate situation involving my daughter.
Brian listened attentively and was able to comfort and advise me. This was what he would consistently do for me for the next two years. Brian was always available, considerate, trustworthy, professional, and compassionate. He immediately arranged for conservatorship, medical assistance, and the myriad of legal procedures that would involve a complicated wrongful death case against the City of Los Angeles. Brian was always kind to my daughter as well as sensitive to the impact she would go through from having her father in a coma and ultimately his death. Brian and Mark were always professional and available when answering my never ending questions or clarifying the legal process.
It's important for me to say that Brian changed our lives when everything went dark. He gently guided us for two years through this experience. The case was settled and we found resolution. My daughter and I can now continue the process to heal from our devastating loss with resilience and strength.
When you work with Brian and his tireless team, you will understand why people say he is truly the best of the best. My daughter and I are both forever grateful to work with Brian. He continues to be there for us.

BREIT, BREITER, BREITEST
Finding Brian Breiter was like finding that pot of gold at the end of the rainbow. He is an L.A. treasure in the world of litigation and personal injury. All of the reviews contained on this page are 100% right on and accurate and express all of my same sentiments. If I were to elaborate, I would only be "gushing", however, that is exactly what I would like to do. If you find yourself in that awful predicament of being in an auto collision (I use that description rather than "accident"), call Brian..asap. This guy is cool, calm, collected, caring, cohesive, and oh so focused! He slam-dunked my case is 3 months.
About my case: the day after my birthday on Aug. 16th I made a completely legal, clean left turn, when this woman ran a red light and slammed into me so hard, she t-boned my car and made me spin out onto the sidewalk. Yep, my car was totaled. I had another attorney who didn't work out, and then I found Brian Breiter..the "man". I called him in October and met with him at his office around the middle of Oct.
Fast forward to January 22, 2014, when Brian called to tell me that everything was settled and to drop by his office to pick up my check! All those doctor visits and physical therapy and aches and pains had been taken care of and now I had my settlement. I don't ever want to go through such an ordeal again, but if I do, the first call I make will be to Brian J. Breiter..The Breitest star on Wilshire Blvd.

And let me strongly adivse anyone out there who has a similar unfortunate incident occur, don't hesitate, don't wait, pick up the phone and call BRIAN J. BREITER!!! Just do it..you will be happy you did.

I have nothing but great things to say about Mark, Brian, and the rest of the team. They know exactly what they are doing, were incredibly professional, and most of all, they cared. I didn't have to bug them or even communicate much because they were upfront about the timeline  and I knew they were taking care of things.

I was involved in an accident with an out of state driver that resulted in me tearing my ACL and needing 2 different surgeries to fix it. He ran a stop sign and just didn't see me. After the first conversation with their insurance adjustor to be paid out for the total loss of my car, I knew it was going to be a pain dealing with them because they were bickering over the $100 or so that I wanted to replace my tinted windows. I started to imagine what it would be like dealing with them over the phone with 10s or 100s of thousands of dollars on the line.

That's when I hired Mark and Brian to handle everything and that they did. I was rest assured knowing that the insurance company would respect that and their firm as they bring wrongful death cases to trail. Knowing that allowed me to focus 100% on the recovery and rehab. It took over 3.5 years for everything to settle and I'm confident in saying that they helped me receive quite a far larger settlement than I believe I would have been able to get on my own or elsewhere.

They are the real deal. I feel privileged that they were able to work with me. They aren't some massive shop that just takes every case they can.  They were responsive, strategic, smart, upfront,  and not once did I feel like just another number. They cared about my health and treated me as a friend the entire way through. They don't take everyone, but If they say they'll bring you on as a client, I'd absolutely recommend it ASAP.

On October 27, 2015 my brother was killed in a tragic accident.  He was riding his bike home from the beach.  A semi with a shipping container was traveling the same way as he was, he was going under a bridge at  the same time as the semi with the shipping container. As the semi made its  way under the bridge the load was too high, the shipping container clipped the top of the bridge and fell onto my brother as he was riding his bike and killed him instantly.  My brother was my everything, he was the only one left out of my immediate family.  I’ll never forget the night, the coroner came to my house as soon as he introduced himself as the coroner I knew that my brother was dead.  I remember all my family coming over that night and the next morning there was all different news vans sitting in front of my house channel 7 channel 9 channel 4.etc...  they were pounding on my door at 7:30 8 o’clock in the morning, I never once gave the news a statement, I was too distraught so my cousins would always tell them to go away.  Then I had a lot of lawyers waiting in front of my house because they wanted my business, I also had a couple of shady characters trying to get me to hire them to represent my brothers case, when I say shady I mean they were trying to do anything and everything to get me to hire them. At this point I did not know what to do so I started researching lawyers and Brian’s name came up.  His name came up actually several times with 100% success rate.  I finally met with Brian and my family was telling me that I should meet with at least three more lawyers to get a second and third opinion.  At this time I really didn’t feel like meeting with different lawyers I just didn’t have the energy I felt so drained from everything.  After I met with Brian my cousins and I went to go have some Pho, and on the fortune cookie that I got it said your first choice will be the wisest  to follow at this time.  After that fortune cookie I knew that Brian was my first choice and he was the wisest choice.  So I highly recommend Brian he’s one of the best trial lawyers around or to me is the best!  2 1/2 years later it was bittersweet but my brothers case was finally settled and Brian did an amazing job even though there’s no amount of money that can bring my brother back, Brian made sure that I got the max amount.  Thank you Brian Brieter you’re the best!

Brian Breiter, Thank you and your team for all your efforts. Thank you for making the painful and stressful process very relieving. Two years ago when I told a few friends that my lawyer was "Super Lawyer, Brian Breiter ", they told me, ahh you'll never even get him on the phone. Well every time I called, you picked up. So that is much appreciated. And Congratulations on the opening of the new office in Miami.
PS I still want a rematch at Backgammon.

Brian Breiter is an awesome lawyer and a really great guy. I was a pedestrian crushed between two cars and suffered a severe injury. I called Brian up, and he came to see me in the hospital shortly after I had my accident. I was hurt really bad and he assured me that in time I'd be ok. He explained the process of my case to me, and answered any questions that I had. He was really there for my recovery.  He got the case settled in a timely manner. And when it was all said and done I got a good deal money. I greatly appreciate all the hard work that he a n his team did and I'd recommend if you need a great attorney to hire Brian Breiter.

Many years ago, when Brian Brieter was in Law School, a mutual acquaintance of ours commented that Brian was going to be a great Lawyer. The person making the comment was a prominent member of our community who knew lawyers. I saw the person making the comment recently and reminded him of what he had said about Brian back in the day. We both are really impressed about Brian's very successful law career and the legions of people he had helped over the years.
Every time I need a lawyer I ask Brian what he thinks and he very carefully takes the time to listen and advise me. It impresses me that very often he has advised me for free. In addition, He has referred me to other lawyers because it was away from his area of expertise.
Brian very clearly lays out a road map and strategy with different alternatives,  makes sure to put you at ease and assure you that things will be alright.
Brian is tenacious, bright, thoughtful and a great communicator. He has an uncanny ability to keep things light with his sense of humor yet he is very serious about achieving his clients goals!
I am not easily impressed, but I highly recommend Brian Brieter to anyone needing legal advice!

I have worked closely with Brian and his fellow lawyers. They do amazing work and have helped my patients get compensated for their injuries. I take emergency room call and have many trauma patients who ask for a referral to a lawyer to help them. I work with many lawyers because of my work at level 1 trauma centers, and they are by far the best. Patient reported settlement and jury awarded results that are significantly higher. Recently, I also had a patient get injured and had a grossly disfigured nose. Her insurance denied her treatment at the time of her injury, wanted her to follow up and get treatment at the insurance facility. They guaranteed that she would be expeditiously seen and treated. She was discharged from the emergency room and called her insurance right away. They told her that they wouldn't be able to see her for a week. She contacted Brian Breiter, he referred her to me without knowing that I had seen her the day before, and arranged to have her surgery with me to fix her broken nose the next day. Brian and his fellow lawyers are talented, and take care of their clients by arranging for both the best medical care and getting them the maximum monetary compensation possible. I would not go to anyone else if I were injured and was looking for a good lawyer.

I was driving on my way to work last June when a ride sharing service car cut me off trying to make a right turn from the middle lane, struck my car and ultimately ending up pushing me into another vehicle. My car  was deemed totaled and I was at a loss at what steps to take next. After a recommendation from a coworker to pursue legal action I was introduced to Brian.

From our very first meeting I was treated with nothing but kindness and respect. I had never been in an accident before and really felt quite helpless. I was deemed "ok" by the medics when they arrived but was still in severe pain after the accident. It was only because of Brian's recommendation that I got an MRI- turns out I had several herniated discs in my lower back!

Brian and his whole team took care of setting me up with the best orthopedic, PT team, and massage therapist. They truly were focused on me getting better and always checked in throughout the whole process anytime I had a question or concern. Whether it be by email, phone, or even texting on Brians personal cell they were always available to me.

When I first met with Brian he was confident that he would win my case and gave me an idea of what my settlement amount could be. Well not only did he WIN but he more than doubled the initial amount AND was able to reduce ALL of my medical bills.

This whole process (minus the accident) was a breeze! Brian will forever be my personal injury lawyer and I would highly recommend his services to anyone!

In October of 2013, I was crossing the street near my own home in LA when I was hit by an SUV.

Nothing can prepare you for something like that. The is no step by step guide for what to do next.

All I knew is I needed help. I needed someone to help me go through this as the car that hit me did not call for help and did not report what happened. The driver also did not give me any information other than a name and phone number which went straight to voicemail every time I tried calling.

I was clearly hurt, I was limping and falling and since no one arrived at the scene when I was hurt, I was basically advocating for myself, which I found out, is VERY difficult to do after such a traumatic event.

Before I found Brian and Marc, I found myself being sent home from the er  hospital and doctor multiple times with a pat on the back, letting me know it is painful to get by a car but I would feel better soon since I am younger than 40.

This dance lasted until my own health insurance had enough complaints to referred me to an orthopedic doctor. I finally got an appointment to see the orthopedic doctor a week after thanksgiving. The moment the orthopedic doctor laid eyes on me, he inquired why no one was helping me go through this.

I didn't even realize what I was going through was wrong. I didn't even realize I needed help.

My orthopedic doctor let me know I needed 3 MRIs. The sooner the better.

Unfortunately, since I was going through my own insurance, the doctor informed me I could receive the referrals for MRIs maybe by January of the following year. I bursted into tears in the orthopedic doctor's office. I already wasn't sleeping because of the pain. My body was no longer my own. No one seemed to care.

I knew I needed help. That evening, in my desperation, I turned to Yelp after googling for the best lawyer in LA.

When I found Brian Breiter's page, every review I read was truthful and positive. The more reviews I read, the more it became clear: this was a team who cared.

I figured that my situation couldn't get any worse that what happened that day in the orthopedic doctor's office. I took a chance and I called them.

I am so glad I did. Although I was scared, hurt, confused, unsure of what to do, they believed me and were willing to meet with me.

I walked into Brian's office, limping with 2 young children in tow. The minute I saw Brian and Marc, they believed me and were willing to represent me.

15 minutes later, I was sent in an uber to a doctor and I got immediate care. It turned out I had real issues with my neck, back, my arms and my left leg. I had definitely needed more than just home care.

By that evening, a plan to care for my injuries and to recover from them was set in motion.

For the next FOUR YEARS, we talked about my care every step of the way. Anytime I called, anyone I spoke to knew who I was. Every time I called, the call always ended with "Don't worry about anything. Focus on getting better" and Brian absolutely delivered.  I finally got the medical care I had desperately needed.

Brian and Marc saw me for what I was: a young wife and mother of 2 young children whose lives have changed from this accident. The compassion and understanding is something that definitely made a difference to my family and I.

Although nothing in the world can ever fix the pain I have or replace the time I lost as I continuing to recover from this accident, Brian and Marc made sure justice was delivered to my family and I.

There are not enough words to express how grateful I am to have found a lawyer like Brian. My family and I whole lives are much better because Brian is willing to be someone who fights for what's fair. Thank you Brian and Marc!

Brian is a great lawyer. A few years ago I was on my way to standup gig a benefit for the AA club in Orange. While en route through Cypress I was a victim of a hit and run drunk driver, the irony is not lost on me.
My car was totaled and I was left unconscious, injured and in need of medical attention. After I was released from the hospital I immediately called Brian. Brian took a horrifically traumatic moment in my life and made it a lot easier. He managed the insurance, hospital and ambulance phone calls and worked diligently to resolve my case fast. Since then, whenever anyone is in need of a personal injury attorney I don't hesitate to refer them to Brain, simply because he's the best and gets results. I hope no one has to go through what I went through, but if you do leave it up to Brian he's a professional.

Thank you Brian Breiter. Thank you for your concern. For your personal care. For your attentiveness. For your expertise. Thank you for making a terribly unfortunate incident in my life less terrible. I was bitten in the face by a Bull Mastiff. For those of you who do not know what that is it's a very large dog. He took a chunk right out of my face. I bled horribly and was rushed to the ER where I received well over 100 stitches in my face. I was referred to Mr Breiter and his team by a longtime friend who I deem trustworthy and knowledgable. After arranging what seemed to be almost an immediate meeting the day after the incident Brian formulated a precision game plan, recommended an evaluation of my face by a top plastic surgeon, and also asked if I thought I might need any kind of therapy to assist me in what could possibly be a fear of dogs in the future. He coached me well for the deposition. He went head to head with the heartless insurance company representing the dog's owner who was careless. Brian fought hard for me. I remember being so impressed by the amount of respect he commanded for the defendant's lawyer. Brian has swagger. He's got style. He's got the know-how to get the job done. He did not rest until all of my bills were paid and I walked away with a very nice settlement. I definitely have a scar to show for it but as they say, when life gives you lemons, call Brian Breiter. I'd give 100 stars if I could. Seriously.
reviews.ownerResponse: Jason, you are one of the bravest clients I’ve ever had. You also have the best attitude for someone who went through such a horrible ordeal. You are very lucky that the surgeons were able to do such a good job, and that you still are devastatingly handsome! You have become a great friend throughout this process and I treasure our relationship.
Thank you
Thanks

reviews.text:
Jonathan Blecher is a miracle worker. I was arrested for a DUI in January 2025, did not blow and was in the middle of a trauma blackout wherein I wasn’t the most pleasant person with the police. The case would have probably lost at trial.

I was defending myself, and Jonathan reached out when I contacted several referral services.

(The other attorney who contacted me through the referral service was a jerk from Sarasota who belittled me and tried pressuring me to give him  upwards of $8,500)

Although climbing Everest might have been easier than convincing a jury this arse was actually innocent- we decided to give it a shot.

For a very reasonable fee, Jonathan was able to get the charge reduced to a reckless, 6 mo probation, and no license resuspension.

I am a tough pill to swallow at times, but Jonathan stuck with me and made a miracle happen. He even quipped that he’s never had a client quite like me.

Jonathan is professional, and has the experience of being a past ASA. I recommend him wholeheartedly.
reviews.ownerResponse: I really appreciate your kind words, Chris, and so pleased with the outcome for you.

reviews.text: This guy is legit. Most lawyers that do dui cases are trying to rip you for an arm and a half and dont care about you. I can testify that this is not the case for Blecher. He provided honest and fair advice and I would not have been able to fight through this without him!!
reviews.ownerResponse: I'm so thankful for your kind words and glad I was able to help you.

Being prompt and knowledgeable, he answered my questions to my relief.
I would recommend Jonathan because he went out of his way to give me guidance without looking for anything in return.
Thank you *****

reviews.text: Jonathan was amazing.  He answered a late evening call and helped assist me with a very troubling set of circumstaces.  He worked on related matters for several hours thereafter.  If there was a 100 star rating he would be 100/100.  Thanks again Jonathan!

reviews.text: Horrible firm . Should apply as an elected official for the state attorneys office ! Couldn’t even hear me out . He’s looking for easy money and quick plea !!!!!

I suspect that you might be the person I very briefly spoke with yesterday morning but I told you I’m not taking cases like yours any longer and nothing else. That was the extent of our conversation. reviews.text: Mr. Blecher was very approachable and patiently answered my questions. I ended the conversation feeling informed. I truly appreciate his professional and courteous advice.

reviews.text: Very rude and prejudice!

I have no idea who you are, what you look like or where you're from. I said nothing to you other than identify myself by name- you did all the talking after that. How you can insult me by dropping the word "prejudice" is ridiculous, irresponsible and wrong. Please seek help.

reviews.text: Jonathan took the time to call me and review my options as well as give me advice. He spent over 30 minutes talking to me and answered all my questions. He didn't charge me for his time either! He was very knowledgeable with florida laws and was very helpful!!

If you are considering hiring an attorney, you definitely need to contact him and give him an opportunity to look into your case.

reviews.text:
Consultation with Former Judge David Kwangsoo Kim
I paid $500 for a one-hour consultation with former judge David Kwangsoo Kim regarding my immigration case.
I sent all of my case documents in advance, but during the consultation, it felt like he had not reviewed or understood my materials at all. There was no sign of preparation.

Because of his background as a former judge, I expected deeper insight and a different perspective than what typical immigration attorneys provide. However, the consultation was very superficial, with little practical strategy or concrete guidance.
In fact, it felt less helpful than consultations I’ve had with immigration attorneys who charge $100–$150 per hour.
In particular, his understanding of current immigration practice and practical, hands-on experience seemed quite limited.

Most of the consultation focused on negative statements like “it will be difficult” or “it may not work,” without offering realistic options or strategic direction. Overall, the value did not justify the cost.

After the session, I shared my concerns about the lack of substance for a $500/hour consultation. I was told that his usual rate is $1,000 per hour and that $500 was a “discount.” However, regardless of the stated rate, the level of preparation, depth of analysis, and practical expertise did not feel appropriate for such a high fee.

I went in expecting meaningful insight because of his former judge title, but the experience was disappointing.

I am so grateful for attorneys Heather Roberts and Helena Tetzeli. I had a very complicated case, but they both navigated the process meticulously and got me the best result possible. They communicated regularly and were very honest and upfront about the costs and didn't bill me later for more money as other attorneys have tried to in the past. I have since referred two other people to their firm and if anyone is reading this, I highly recommend this firm to you. Thank you again, Heather and Helena!

Thank you to the firm for an incredible, transparent, and trustworthy partnership on my family’s journey to obtaining green card status! The process was super simple and easy. Odalys is a super star associate that was super hands-on with our legal needs and was always early to provide guidance.

In this age of high scrutiny around immigration, Mrs. Tetzeli and team is the strategic edge families need. 10/10 service.

I retained John Pratt for a marriage-based immigration case and ended up switching to a new lawyer after about four months. Thankfully, my new attorney successfully obtained my green card.

Here are some of the major issues I experienced:
1.	Lack of progress and multiple errors: After nearly four months, there was little to no progress on my case, and I discovered several mistakes in the application materials they prepared. (As a recent law school graduate myself, I was able to identify these errors.)
2.	Overpriced for the quality of service: The firm charged well above the market rate, which I initially assumed reflected higher-quality legal work—but that was not the case. They were slow to respond to questions, unhelpful on key issues, and overall did not provide competent representation.
3.	Issues with refund process: When I terminated the contract, it took nearly a month to receive my refund because they repeatedly made errors with my bank information. They never apologized and even attempted to place the blame on me, despite my providing correct details.
4.	Refusal to provide work product: Upon termination, I requested copies of all work product related to my case. The firm refused, claiming ownership of the documents. This is inconsistent with professional responsibility rules, which make clear that clients are entitled to their own case files.

Overall, I would not recommend this firm based on my experience.

Taylor Alicia Hill is an incredible attorney. She is extremely knowledgeable and will go out of her way for you to get you the information needed to win your case. She is very savvy and understands the law better than most attorneys. She will not sugarcoat the merits of your case and if she cannot help you she will point you in the direction of who can. Do not hesitate to get a heavy hitter like her on your side. Thank you so much Taylor!

I am deeply grateful to my immigration attorneys Keven Gregg, Elizabeth Montano, and John Pratt, for helping my family through a complex and emotionally challenging immigration process. We were facing a difficult family-based green card case, and from the very beginning, they provided expert legal guidance, clear communication, and genuine compassion. They accompanied us to the green card interviews, ensuring we were fully prepared and supported throughout the process.

When our case encountered unexpected issues — including a flawed initial decision by USCIS — our attorney didn’t give up. They thoroughly reviewed the case, prepared a strong appeal through AAO, and stood by us every step of the way. They prepared extremely high-quality legal documentation — thorough, well-organized, and tailored specifically to the unique details of our situation. Their attention to detail made a clear difference in the final decision.

Thanks to their dedication, professionalism, and deep knowledge of immigration law, we were able to resolve the situation and finally achieve a positive outcome. I wholeheartedly recommend them to anyone navigating complex immigration challenges.

It is RARE that you find a professional who is not only competent but passionate about what they do. Odalys was extremely detailed, professional, responsive, and experienced. She stayed with us throughout our entire process and was responsive every step of the way, including the interview. Thank you for all of your assistance, care, professionalism, and initiative. This is an all-star law firm, and I highly recommend their immigration services.

To be candid, the experience left us feeling not only disheartened but also questioning the value of the meeting altogether. The attorney arrived 15 minutes late and did not engage with us for the full hour we had scheduled. More importantly, throughout the meeting, there was a consistent lack of empathy and helpfulness.

Every time we asked a question, the response was along the lines of: “If you hired me, I’d do this, but that comes at a cost $20,000.” It felt less like a consultation and more like a sales pitch. We came to the meeting as a working family, seeking at least a basic level of guidance in exchange for the fee we paid. Instead, we were met with transactional responses that left us demoralized and without any real sense of direction.

We understand that legal services come at a cost, but a consultation should offer at least some foundational advice or reassurance. What we received felt cold, dismissive, and not reflective of the professionalism we had hoped for.

We left the meeting feeling like we had wasted our money, and worse, our hope.

I hope you’ll take this feedback seriously, as we believe no family should walk away from a legal consultation feeling more confused, disappointed, and discouraged than when they arrived. This is a serious issue, not a sales pitch.

We are very grateful with KKTP for taking on our case and helping us fulfill our dream of becoming legal residents of this great country that welcomed us 25 years ago. Thank you, Attorney Clayton, for helping us and walking with us through this process.
Thank you for giving hope to many families who are currently stuck between a rock and a hard place.
God bless you and may you continue to be instruments of justice in every case that comes to your office.
I recommend this Law Office KKTP

My relationship with KKTP began with my application for a U.S. immigrant visa when the COVID-19 pandemic hit, and ended just a few days ago with me becoming a naturalized citizen of the U.S. I cannot adequately express in words my gratitude for the incredible legal support and guidance I received from them during this journey.

Yes, I probably could have navigated the applications myself and saved some money. I’m confident, however, that it was absolutely the correct decision to use KKTP. Their expert guidance ensured that I didn’t have to second guess what U.S. immigration laws required of me when submitting my applications and attending my interviews, and that I could thus avoid making any inevitable mistakes along the way that likely come with self-representation. I am thankful to Heather Roberts, in particular, but also Clayton Oates, for their flawless representation.

The Kurzban Kurzban Tetzeli & Pratt team fought my case for years before USCIS, and, when it became necessary, in federal court.  The agency did not want to listen.  The team did everything they could to win a U.S. citizenship case that I have been fighting, in some form, since 1996.

Attorney Ira Kurzban is not just an experienced lawyer, he is an ACE in every aspect of immigration matters. Attorney Ira personally dove into my case to find solutions. I am very happy with their services, and am pleased to say that I am now finally a U.S. citizen thanks to their efforts!

Ira’s partner Attorney Kevin A. Gregg took my case very seriously as he narrowed down the issues, researched to get positive results, and kept fighting. Kevin’s personal touch with detail on my case (for years) resulted in a very favorable decision from a federal judge in Florida, which turned the table for USCIS to settle the case. Attorney Kevin negotiated with US attorneys for the settlement.

I have great respect for our country and the State Department and Department of Homeland Security. At the end of the day, having a great law firm on my side brought closure to my case, and helped the agencies see the truth.  I was persistent and understanding of our judicial system. I have gone through several one-on-one interviews with State Department and USCIS, with over 1000 pages of documents submitted. Finally, in January 2025, I took the oath and officially became a U.S. Citizen. This put an end to my 35 years of struggle. I stayed steadfast in my belief and fought tirelessly through the legal system.

KKTP stayed with me as I tried to then obtain a U.S. passport. After attending an interview in Miami, it appeared that my application was put on hold. This is when Ira’s Partner Attorney Carl Risch from the Washington D.C. office jumped in. Attorney Carl Risch was able to get everything fixed.  I finally received my U.S. Passport in February 2025.

Thank you all from the bottom of my heart at Kurzban Kurzban Tetzeli & Pratt, P.A

I am very pleased with your representation, commitment, and I highly and strongly recommend your firm to anyone for any hard to solve complex cases.

reviews.text:
We recently had the pleasure of working with Jim Nosich and his amazing team, and I can confidently say that they exceeded our expectations in every way. From the very beginning, we were struck by their kindness, empathy, and willingness to listen to our concerns.

Jim himself was an absolute pleasure to work with. He took the time to get to know our family and their situation, and he always made us feel like we were his top priority. He was incredibly knowledgeable about malpractice law, and he helped us understand the complexities of the case in a way that was clear and easy to understand.

But Jim's greatness doesn't stop there. His team is simply wonderful. Sharon, in particular, brought such positive energy to every interaction we had. Her high positive vibes were infectious, and she always made us feel like everything was going to be okay.

And Sonia, the paralegal, was JUST amazing. Her loving kindness and motherly touch were exactly what we needed during such a difficult time. She was always there to answer our questions and provide reassurance when we needed it most.

But perhaps the thing I appreciated most about Jim and his team was their clear communication. They were always available to talk, and they never left us wondering about the status of the case. They truly made the entire process so easy to deal with, and their empathy and understanding of our situation made all the difference.

Overall, Jim Nosich and his team were an absolute pleasure to work with. Despite dealing with a horrible situation, they made the family feel supported and understood every step of the way. Their empathy, kindness, and problem-solving skills are unmatched, and I would highly recommend them to anyone in need of a malpractice attorney. Thank you, Jim, Sharon, Sonia, and the entire team, for your wonderful work

A very bad experience with unethical attorney during a real estate transaction.  Craig M Dorne, Esq. not only lost a real estate deal for his client during the sale of their commercial property in Miami but he was trying to steal buyers' deposit delaying and obstructing the return of their money and overbilling their own clients creating a legal problem where is no one. Be sure to do your Due Diligence before hiring an attorney. Never hire an attorney who is not Bar Real Estate Certified. It can backfire on you if you don't do your Due Diligence and hire somebody who is totally incompetent and has zero experience in real estate.

We are filling a complaint with The Florida Bar and working on our malpractice lawsuit against this totally dishonest, unethical and lawyer who should be disbarred immediately because of his malpractice and unethical dealings. It is a shame when you find this kind of attorney. It is not usual, but unfortunately, it can happen. we worked and know a lot of attorneys in Miami, that are real professionals and are always trying to do the best for everybody involved in the transaction and the most important they follow the law and the Code of Ethics.
We never would recommend this attorney to anybody.

If you want more detailed information about the practice of this attorney to feel free to contact us anytime.

He stuck a computer in front of her and pointed where to sign.  There was no grant.  It was a horrifying 32 page lease contract for 25 years for $75,000! The solar company demanded $3500 just to cancel! Christian did a great job getting this cancelled without fees from the solar company. Thank you Christian for your prompt responses and skill to exit this nightmare.

We had a very positive experience with the Gilchrist Law Firm in handling our legal matters. They were professional, yet made us feel very at ease. Mr. Christian Granados was exceptional on handling our case promptly. Would highly recommend Gilchrist Law Firm PA.

Christian Granados has been incredible to work with. He is extremely communicative and does so in a way that’s easy to understand. He speaks to you like an equal and truly has your best interests at heart. Hopefully I won’t need his services again, but if I do, I will absolutely call him!

This company was the best that can happen to me. I sold and bought my property with them. Closings can be really difficult and stressful. I worked with Alejandra Reyes. She is a wonderful prosesor. She advised me what to do and help me with all my concerns. She knows the business, anticipate to any possible problem and fixed it in advanced. She is super patient, and I was impressed  with her professional skills. I hardly recommend her and the company.

I’ve done multiple closings with them. I can’t say enough good things. They are very responsive and helpful along the way. My experience has been amazing specially dealing with Alejandra Reyes. She has always been supportive and efficient, her diligence has assist in the stressful process that a closing can become always finding solutions and assisting anyway she can. I recommend their services, and I hope you’re able to work with Alejandra as she’s such an incredible asset and professional. Always a pleasure to deal with. Thank you!

Had a small contract dispute and Christian Granados and the Gilchrist Law firm agreed to help me with it. Christian researched the matter and provided the right guidance that ultimately brought around a favorable settlement. I really appreciated his professionalism and that they were willing to stand behind the little guy!

Reached out to this firm from a legal referral service. Upon my first contact with the firm, I was informed I would receive a call from counsel within 24 hrs. A week later, I received an email explaining this request was outside their level of expertise. Unfortunately, this was a time sensitive matter involving basic contractual concepts in real estate. I did give them a star due to the fact there was a follow up, although it was not punctual.

Christian was a wonderful talented attorney who quickly developed a plan of action for our case. He had our case going by the end of the week. We are now receiving the funds requested and happy to have his expertise. I highly recommend Christian for any real estate issues you may have.

I am very pleased with the skill and kindness I received from your lawyers and assistant in this case, Cristián and Nicolás, because they were very concerned about my case. And I am grateful for the way they stood up against the injustice to which we were exposed. I will keep them in mind when recommending this firm to others who need legal support. Thank you for everything.

Unfortunately, I cannot recommend Felicetti Law Firm. I have been completely ghosted—no returned phone calls, no email responses, and no updates on my case.

I’ve completed my physical therapy, and the party at fault has already sent correspondence requesting follow-up, but I’ve heard nothing from my own legal team.

Clients deserve basic communication and accountability, and that has been missing here.

NEVER get involved with these lawyers; they're scammers. All they do is collect the chiropractor's money, then they never answer your calls, and we never found out what happened to our case. They're crooks, and they have different people answer the phone, and whoever does say they have nothing to do with it... but when they want you to choose them, they're all right. They call every day, but then they disappear. We NEVER heard from them again.

The entire team treated me with respect and compassion while remaining focused and results-driven. They were well-prepared, honest, and transparent about expectations. I always felt informed and supported. Their commitment to achieving the best possible result truly impressed me. I would not hesitate to recommend them to friends or family.

While searching for law firms for a necessary procedure, these same firms keep popping up, and after reading the reviews, I realize nothing has changed. They're still ripping people off. Last year, my husband, my mother, and my granddaughter were in an accident. They made us go to therapy sessions an hour away from home with the promise that they would find us a place closer to my house, but they never did. Then it was impossible to contact them; they never answered, and when they did, they said I had to wait. Finally, after four months of chasing them for any information, they told me they hadn't found anything. All I accomplished was wasting my time and money going to those therapy sessions. It's disrespectful to play with people like that.

The team was organized, communicative, and focused on achieving positive results. They made a complex legal situation manageable and less stressful. Their professionalism and dedication truly set them apart from other firms.

No one answers
Case been open for over two years. No follow through no call back. Waste of time and money. Fake reviews look at the dates they see all posted.

Law Firm Felicetti delivers high-quality legal services with a personal touch. I felt respected and heard throughout my case. Their ability to explain complex legal matters in simple terms was incredibly helpful. This firm truly prioritizes its clients and provides dependable legal guidance.

The team handled my case efficiently while keeping me informed throughout the process. Their expertise, responsiveness, and dedication were evident from start to finish. It’s a firm that truly values its clients and works diligently to protect their interests.

This is utter garbage. They claim to be professionals, they never answer the phone. I've been waiting over a year to collect my compensation. The case manager, Elizabeth, is useless, incredibly rude and unprofessional. These people are worthless.

Their attorneys handled every detail with professionalism and care, keeping me informed throughout the process. Their strategic thinking and strong understanding of the law were evident at every stage. They treat clients with respect and prioritize clear communication. A highly dependable firm for anyone needing strong legal guidance and organized representation.

They provide clear direction, realistic expectations, and consistent follow-through, which creates a sense of trust from the beginning. Their dedicated attorneys work hard to simplify complicated matters and keep clients informed every step of the way. Exceptional service and a highly committed approach make them stand out.

Terrible experience! DO NOT HIRE THEM. My accident happened over 6 years ago, the insurance company paid me a long time ago, but they don't respond, they lie, they don't return calls, they don't answer, they don't give any explanation, and they have a complete lack of professional ethics. I've gone and called many times, and it's always lies! Can anyone help us with this?

Their team takes the time to listen, understand concerns, and propose solutions tailored to each situation. I found their guidance steady, well-researched, and practical. They truly prioritize the client experience, making the entire legal journey far more reassuring. A great choice for anyone looking for thoughtful legal support.

My name is Rosa do not hire this attorney a higher than a year ago and I haven't received no calls no message they're trying to rob me is the worst attorney in Miami do not hire these people it's a big mistake I'm having problems already trying to collect my money from these people they're unprofessional and they're no good they still have not given my money do not hire them

Their attorneys provided strategic advice, thorough explanations, and ongoing updates that kept everything organized and easy to understand.
